item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as they're by Young-Deok Seo (as indicated here) with no indication they've been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel the experiment log makes this SCP. Very good stuff. While on that…

One Was always loyal
One Should have known

… are these capitalizations intentional? Unfortunately, though, I have to ÷1 on this story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Based on the pictures I'm guessing you mean "Bicycle Chains" and not "Chain chains"
That is my only comment…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Needs work on tone and clarity.

I like the ending interview. Since I like the last interview, no vote instead of downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ya, I really dont like the second photo either, it just feels tacked on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Nicely written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great photos on this one. I'm not too sure about the first interview; the second one felt more real, interesting and relevant to the article overall.

I got a little jumbled in the middle of the description of what all it does, though. The writing could use tightening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does seems awfully interested in knots, doesn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, am I right in thinking the Storm was the Breath?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some SCPs that use a photo of an art sculpture and manage to make it seem real and mysterious when writing about it. Unfortunately, this one doesn't do that for me. Especially the second photo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no recovery log, per say, just hints.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chains

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well said. I definitely agree. Maybe it's that buildup you describe that I'm hungering for in this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: blarglegargle see above comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very true. The only usual place that extra expungement can help an article is if someone is flailing around with pseudoscience so transparent you can use it to glaze windows.

Other than that.. yeah. Now, i'll bite the bullet, and tell you that it is indeed possible to redact or expunge in places where you know that what you can write in isn't going to be any better than a redaction (I have done it a fair amount of times).
BUT.
Think of an expungement like a classic movie gag. A bunch of villains surrounds the protagonist with blunt weapons, and he instinctively reaches for a gun in his coat. You might even see its shadow. as he calmly tells them off and counts to three… and then you see it were just his fingers folded into the shape of one.
Now, if he pulled out a hand and yelled "Boom!" it wouldn't have the intended effect, would it?
Redaction, in principle works the same. If you absolutely have to use it because you know you can't do better than leaving a blank space, you need to write the buildup to absolutely persuade whoever's reading that there's something underneath.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
did I miss it, or is there no Recovery Log….I'd like to see one….
I'm upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, execpt for the last one, which was intentional (the past tense, that is).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That used to. That was the point of the article, really- it lost its motivation completely. Something took it away from it. That something got what it wanted from it (the chain knots), and then left it to get captured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just another scary thing that kills people and makes more of itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Couple of items:

In Containment:

"Due to SCP-1770 resistance" should be SCP-1770's.

Same sentence:

"corrosive materials , the" should be "corrosive materials, the".

In Description, Stage Two:

"SCP-1770 was known" should be "SCP-1770 has been known".

In Stage Three:

"SCP-1770 showed a powerful protective urge towards the knot formations, and devoted " should be "SCP-1770 shows a powerful protective urge towards knot formations, and will devote".

Other than these minor corrections, an interesting idea. It didn't really grab me, I guess - it's not what I consider a very thought-provoking SCP, but not every one needs to be a twist ending, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
More vagueness and [REDACTED]/[DATA EXPUNGED] would be welcome here. It's always an important part of upping the creep factor.

Just so you know for future reference, Gall, that's absolutely terrible advice. Countless articles have been shredded here because the author assumed that [DATA EXPUNGED] meant "interesting" or "I don't have to write anything here."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find that I tend to overuse reduction, and too little of it is not nearly as bad as too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good! I really love the concept. But I feel like you gave a little too much away in the description. More vagueness and [REDACTED]/[DATA EXPUNGED] would be welcome here. It's always an important part of upping the creep factor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just awesome, and actually ads to what we saw of the MoC in story, and casts him in a new light.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked the idea in the story, like it even more here and like it most because it's art without AWCY.
